sessions.intent: Plan finishing the [VariableName] migration deferred from runtime2-data-share-state.

before.assumptions: Migration is mechanical once we agree on the name-resolution path. The Data.As<T> contract already propagates Name from %var% form via TryFullVarMatch — both the unset and the live-variable paths land on Data.Name = canonical var name, not slot name.
before.risk: If As<T> Name propagation doesn't actually hold for the live-variable path in all variants, the migration breaks silently — handlers would write to slot name 'Name' instead of var name 'x'. Hence Phase 0 (prove the claim with tests) before any handler change.

actions.context: Phased plan: Phase 0 lock-in tests, Phase 1 read sites, Phase 2 write sites with IBuildValidatable, Phase 3 delete Legacy/__StripPercent/[VariableName], Phase 4 docs update. Conversation with Ingi validated the design; existing NamePropagationTests already prove the core claim but a JSON-driven variable.set test was requested to nail down the full path.

after.status: Plan written. Phase 0 hand-off to coder is the next step.
after.notes: Branch created from runtime2 at 4a4a0f6a. No code changes yet — handoff to coder for Phase 0 tests.

sessions.intent: Verify architect's Phase 0 claim (Data.As<T> propagates .Name='x' from value='%x%') and report findings.

before.assumptions: Mechanism is plausible — TryFullVarMatch + ConstructWrap propagate Name through As<T> recursion. Need real tests on the exact slot Data shape from .pr deserialization to lock the contract. Ingi explicitly does not trust the claim and wants proof before any handler migration.
before.risk: If the bare-name case (value='x' no %) silently writes to slot key 'Name', the migration is a robustness regression vs the existing [VariableName] / __StripPercent path.

actions.context: Four TUnit tests covering architect's full claim: var-missing, var-exists, bare-name negative, round-trip. All pass. Documents the As<T> Name-propagation contract whether or not the migration ships.

Read As<T> / AsT_Impl / TryFullVarMatch / ConstructWrap to trace the Name flow manually. Confirmed generator line 50 emits __ResolveData(name).As<T>(Context) for Data<T> properties — same call my tests exercise. Confirmed real .pr shape matches the test fixture {name:'Name', value:'%x%'}.
End-to-end run of `set %x% = "hello"` via `plang --test` — [Pass]. Proves the live pipeline works today; mechanism is real, not theoretical.

Session artifacts. architect-handoff.md is the operative file — outlines the design decision (keep [VariableName] canonical, Legacy permanent) and asks for a v2 plan covering read-site migration only.

Ingi decided to keep [VariableName] permanently. Reasoning: it handles both '%x%' and bare 'x' natively via __StripPercent, matching robustness the As<T>.Name path would need IBuildValidatable to recover. Sending plan back to architect for v2.

after.status: Phase 0 verified mechanically (4/4 pass). Migration declined on robustness grounds. Handoff to architect for v2 plan.
after.notes: Architect picks up architect-handoff.md next. Test file retained as documentation of the As<T> Name-propagation contract — not load-bearing for any migration but useful for future drift detection.
